_readme: This file documents the data shape Margin uses. It is not loaded automatically — actual data lives in your browser's localStorage so it persists between visits. Use this file as a reference for the JSON structure, or as a starting template: rename it, fill in real entries, and use the Import button in the app to load it in.

entry._description: One row in the submissions table.
entry.id: string — unique id, auto-generated by the app, leave blank on import and one will be assigned
entry.status: string — one of: applied | interview | offer | rejected | withdrawn
entry.resumeVersion: string — must match the 'name' of an entry in the versions array, or leave empty
entry.dateUpdated: string — ISO date of the last status change
entry.notes: string — free text, e.g. referral source, recruiter contact, interview format
entry.posting: string — optional, the full job description text, used to sharpen Claude's per-entry take
entry.claudeNote: string — populated automatically when you paste a saved Claude response back into the app

version._description: A named resume draft you can tag entries with, to compare performance. Can optionally have a resume file attached, viewable on its own detail page.
version.name: string — short label, e.g. 'Semiconductor-focused v2'
version.addedAt: string — ISO timestamp of when the version was created
version.fileType: string or null — 'pdf' | 'text' | null (no file attached)
version.fileName: string or null — original filename, only set when fileType is 'pdf'
version.fileData: string or null — base64-encoded PDF bytes when fileType is 'pdf', or raw resume text when fileType is 'text'
version.fileSizeBytes: number — size of the original file/text in bytes, used for the size-warning UI

exportPayload._description: The shape of the JSON file produced by the Export button, and what Import expects.
exportPayload.exportedAt: string — ISO timestamp of when the export was made

keywordCategoryMap._description: Stored separately under localStorage key 'margin:keywordCategoryMap', not part of Export/Import. Maps a raw extracted keyword (lowercase string) to a cleaner category label, applied to the radar chart. Built via the 'Refine categories with Claude' flow on the main page — generates a prompt, you paste it into a chat with Claude, and paste the JSON response back in to apply it. Example: { "react": "Frontend/React", "aws": "Cloud/AWS" }
